skill.name: daedalus-skill
skill.description: 对话式产品孵化技能。帮助零基础用户将模糊想法转化为结构化产品需求文档（PRD），包含门槛评估、风险提示、竞品对照、边界定义和工具链推荐。
skill.summary: Guided conversational skill that turns vague product ideas into structured PRDs through 12-round dialogue with risk assessment, competitor analysis, and toolchain recommendations.
skill.author: sevenwoood

security_audit.summary: After thorough evaluation, all 179 static findings are false positives. The skill contains only Markdown documentation, conversation flow rules, and prompt templates with no executable code. The 'Ruby/shell backtick' detections are Markdown code fence delimiters (```), not shell execution. Hardcoded URLs point to documentation and reputable tools (skills.sh, github.com, v0.dev, bolt.new). The 'hidden file in home directory' findings reference standard AI tool config directories (~/.claude/skills/) described in installation instructions. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' is a false detection of the Chinese character for 'weak' in a product-design context. High entropy is normal for structured Chinese-language Markdown. This is a safe, documentation-only skill.

low_findings.title: Documentation references to AI tool directories
low_findings.description: The README describes installation paths like ~/.claude/skills/, ~/.cursor/skills/, and ~/.codex/skills/ in its manual installation instructions. These are standard documentation references explaining where users should place the skill files, not actual code that accesses these paths at runtime.

low_findings.title: Outbound URLs in documentation
low_findings.description: Several Markdown files contain URLs to skills.sh, github.com, v0.dev, bolt.new, and changelog.md. These are hyperlinks in documentation, not programmatic network requests. They direct users to external tools and the project's own repository.

content.user_title: Turn Ideas Into Product Plans With Guided Dialogue
content.value_statement: Many people have product ideas but cannot structure them into actionable plans. Daedalus guides non-technical users through a 12-round conversation to produce a complete Product Requirements Document with risk assessment and tool recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through 12 structured conversation rounds to transform vague ideas into clear product concepts
Evaluates project thresholds with three cost paths (lightweight under 500 RMB, medium, full)
Matches user keywords against a built-in competitor library for market context
Outputs compliance and safety risk warnings based on detected feature keywords (location, UGC, payments, health data, minors)
Generates a complete 9-section Markdown PRD with boundary definitions, competitor analysis, and toolchain recommendations
Supports save-and-resume state and degrades to menu mode for minimal user input
limitations: Does not write actual code, create UI designs, or provide legal advice
Risk detection depends on keyword matching and may miss domain-specific or novel risks
Requires a capable model (GPT-4, Claude 3+, Gemini 1.5 Pro+) for best results
Conversation is capped at 12 rounds; complex products may need multiple sessions

use_cases.title: Validate a startup idea before coding
use_cases.description: A first-time founder describes a vague app concept and receives structured guidance on cost, scope, competitors, and risks before committing engineering resources.

use_cases.title: Teach product thinking through dialogue
use_cases.description: A product management instructor uses Daedalus to demonstrate how to elicit requirements, define V1 scope, and assess feasibility through structured questioning.

use_cases.title: Generate a PRD handoff for AI coding tools
use_cases.description: A developer brainstorms with Daedalus to produce a clean Markdown PRD that can be pasted directly into Cursor, v0.dev, or bolt.new to scaffold the initial build.

prompt_templates.title: Start a new product idea session
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to brainstorm a new product idea with you. Ask me your first three open-ended questions about the problem I want to solve.

prompt_templates.title: Request a full risk analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the risks for my current product idea. Show me both compliance/legal risks and technical security risks based on the features we have discussed so far.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to see all accumulated risk warnings

prompt_templates.title: Force PRD generation
prompt_templates.prompt: I am done discussing. Generate the complete PRD now with all 9 sections, toolchain recommendations for both technical and non-technical users, and a closing summary.
prompt_templates.scenario: Ending the session and requesting final output

prompt_templates.title: Save and exit progress
prompt_templates.prompt: Save my progress. Output a snapshot of all state variables (turn count, core idea, likes/dislikes, V1 must-haves, risks reminded) so I can resume later.
prompt_templates.scenario: Pausing the session to return later

output_examples.input: I want to make an app for sharing hidden gem shops. I hate how Xiaohongshu is full of fake influencer spots.
output: Turn 1: Three abstract questions about when you felt the need for this app, what existing products you love, and your ideal first impression on opening it.
Project Threshold Assessment: This product may require backend server/database, map API calls, and cloud storage for image uploads.

Three paths: (1) Lightweight validation under 500 RMB, a few days, no backend. (2) Medium completeness, a few thousand RMB, simple backend like Supabase. (3) Full product, tens of thousands RMB, development team required.
Competitor Reference: Mars (aesthetic but user base too small), Xiaohongshu (large traffic but homogenized), AllTrails (focused on outdoor routes).
Compliance Risk Alert: UGC content requires moderation; location tracking requires explicit user consent under privacy law.
Project Decision: Direction is clear, approved to proceed.
1. Core Pain Point and Scenario: Users want to discover authentic local spots without algorithm-driven influencer content.
2. User Persona: Urban explorers aged 20-35 who value authenticity over trending recommendations.
3. Design Principles: Like editorial curation; dislike algorithmic feed and paid promotion.
4. Feature Boundary: V1 must-have: (a) curated spot list, (b) photo upload, (c) basic map view. Deferred: social feed, reviews. Out of scope: e-commerce integration.
5. Non-functional Requirements: Lightweight path, no backend needed, under 500 RMB.
6. Competitor Comparison: Mars (good design, dead), Xiaohongshu (large but fake), AllTrails (niche).
7. Risk Summary: UGC moderation required, map API cost monitoring.
8. Recommended V0.1 Action: Build a static WeChat mini-program with manually curated shop lists.
9. Toolchain: Pro tools (Figma, Cursor, Supabase); no-code tools (Canva, WeChat Mini Program Cloud, v0.dev).

best_practices: Always answer all three questions in the first round, even briefly, so the skill can build a strong context
When choosing a threshold path, be honest about your budget and timeline to get realistic toolchain recommendations
Explicitly list your V1 must-haves as a numbered list to help the skill enforce the 3-feature boundary rule
anti_patterns: Do not skip the risk analysis step, as the skill uses keyword matching and missing early rounds reduces its detection accuracy
Do not request code generation from this skill; it explicitly does not write code and will redirect you to a toolchain instead
Do not ask for legal advice; the skill provides risk awareness but explicitly disclaims formal legal counsel

faq.question: What AI models work best with Daedalus?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for models with strong instruction following and large context windows, specifically GPT-4, Claude 3, and Gemini 1.5 Pro or higher. Smaller models may produce shallow or inconsistent guidance.

faq.question: Does Daedalus write code or create designs?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly limits itself to product planning and PRD generation. It recommends external tools like Cursor, v0.dev, and Figma for code and design execution.

faq.question: How long is a typical session?
faq.answer: A standard session runs 8 to 12 user turns. The skill automatically ends at 12 turns and outputs a summary PRD. You can also end earlier by saying start or done.

faq.question: Can I save my progress and continue later?
faq.answer: Yes. Say save and exit at any point and the skill will output a Markdown snapshot of all state variables that you can paste back to resume.

faq.question: What languages are supported?
faq.answer: The current version (1.0.7) is in Chinese. English and Japanese versions are listed in the roadmap for v1.1.0.

faq.question: Is the PRD output compatible with AI coding tools?
faq.answer: Yes. The PRD is generated in clean Markdown with tables, lists, and clear section headers. It can be pasted directly into Cursor, v0.dev, bolt.new, or any similar tool that accepts natural language specifications.
